name: Restore PTX Launch Bounds Back To 2-CTA On The Active Hot-Band Path

summary.name: Restore PTX Launch Bounds Back To 2-CTA On The Active Hot-Band Path
summary.hypothesis: The current run already restored the compact PTX hot-band dispatch and preserved the 22016 B shared-memory footprint, but it still trails the accepted PTX base by about 1.50 ms while carrying the exact same 168 regs/thread. The main new lever is __launch_bounds__(128, 3), so rolling that back to 2 should recover scheduler freedom and remove the extra barrier plus short-scoreboard tax without touching grouped rows, stage depth, or the row-pair accumulator path.
summary.expected_bottleneck: The explicit 3-CTA minimum is over-constraining the PTX microkernel schedule, inflating barrier and short-scoreboard even though it does not reduce the measured register footprint.
summary.risk: Low. This is a one-line rollback on the live PTX surface and keeps the current dispatch, shared-memory footprint, and row-pair math path intact.
ranking_notes: This is the cleanest single-variable experiment because the accepted PTX base and the current run share the same 168 regs/thread and 22016 B/block footprint.
The accepted-base comparison points straight at launch policy: barrier is +3.34 pts, short-scoreboard is +5.46 pts, and tensor activity is lower even though occupancy_limit_registers improved to 3.
It preserves the row-pair PTX kernel body that already beat CUTLASS and only removes the new residency forcing lever.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if reverting launch_bounds to 2 does not materially recover barrier or short-scoreboard versus 49dfa799, or if more than the launch-bounds annotation has to change to build.
expected_local_changes: Retune the PTX hot-band __launch_bounds__ annotation from 3 back to 2.
Leave the active PTX dispatch in place.
Leave grouped rows, shared-memory stage depth, and export scratch untouched in this validation pass.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
